finding: `project/` is reserved for authoritative governance only, but current contents include audits, runtime diagnosis, parking-lot notes, and open questions.
current_rule: `project/` should contain authoritative project definitions only.
target_rule: Keep governance only in `project/`; move audit/notes elsewhere.
recommended_action: Move non-authoritative files to `docs/` subtrees and leave only durable contracts in `project/`.

finding: Observed file count exceeds the stated cap for governance files.
current_rule: Maximum governance files allowed: 9.
target_rule: Target policy does not change this; it benefits from a smaller governance surface.
recommended_action: Reduce `project/` to core contracts and move supporting notes to `docs/`.

finding: The written run ID contract requires `run_YYYYMMDD_HHMM_<hash>_<suffix>`, so time-of-day and descriptive suffix are mandatory today.
current_rule: Current regex requires date, time, 7-char hash, and suffix.
target_rule: Top-level bucket should be `YYYYMMDD_<short_hash>` with no time component.
recommended_action: Replace old run ID grammar for future results buckets and decouple descriptive meaning into local explanation files.

finding: Current written rules conflict: lineage docs allow nested `<child_run_id>` folders under a run root, while naming docs forbid repeating `run_id` or timestamp/hash fragments below a run root.
current_rule: Allow `lineage/children/<ordered_role>/<child_run_id>/` but forbid nested repeated run IDs.
target_rule: Child folders should be ordinal-plus-cue only, with no nested run IDs.
recommended_action: Resolve the governance contradiction before migration and standardize on ordinal child folders plus `RUN_CONTEXT.md`.

finding: Top-level `data/results/` contains loose review surfaces and loose artifacts, not only governed run entry points and registries.
current_rule: Top level should be an entry-point layer, not a dumping ground.
target_rule: Target policy still requires clean top-level bucket semantics.
recommended_action: Move loose review surfaces and artifacts under governed namespaces or archive roots.

finding: Existing top-level audit already misses current tree entries because several current names do not satisfy the strict old run ID parser.
current_rule: Audit script classifies only names matching current run ID assumptions.
target_rule: Target policy requires accurate bucket classification under new names.
recommended_action: Update the audit script and regenerate the registry after adopting the new grammar.

finding: Observed top-level run-like directory does not match the current valid run ID regex.
current_rule: Current valid regex is `^run_\d{8}_\d{4}_[0-9a-f]{7}_.+$`.
target_rule: Target bucket would also differ, but should still be governed and explicit.
recommended_action: Treat as historical/supporting non-compliant naming and keep frozen or re-home under archival policy.

finding: Observed top-level run-like directory lacks required time and hash under the current rule.
current_rule: Current run IDs require time and hash.
target_rule: Target bucket removes time but still requires bucketed date-hash grammar.
recommended_action: Leave frozen as historical non-compliant and avoid using as a future model.

finding: Observed name fails current regex because the short hash is not 7 hex chars.
current_rule: Current regex requires 7 lowercase hex characters.
target_rule: Target policy still requires a short hash token, though not necessarily in the same position.
recommended_action: Normalize future names only; leave this lineage historical unless promoted and repaired deliberately.

finding: Current authority semantics are explicit and are the main reason a new naming scheme is feasible.
current_rule: Current `data/results` workflows must resolve by explicit path or `ACTIVE_RUN.json`, not recency.
target_rule: Target policy must preserve explicit active authority.
recommended_action: Keep `ACTIVE_RUN.json` central and expand it if needed rather than reintroducing recency-based discovery.

finding: `runs/latest.txt` is legacy-only for current `data/results` workflows and currently points to a run ID not present under `runs/`.
current_rule: Legacy compatibility helper only; not primary authority.
target_rule: Target policy should not depend on `runs/latest.txt` for results discovery.
recommended_action: Limit `runs/latest.txt` to legacy `runs/` metadata use or retire it after compatibility window.

finding: Observed relative depth reaches 10 levels under one top-level run bucket.
evidence_source: filesystem depth audit performed in this session
current_rule: No explicit numeric depth budget today, but current docs discourage nested run-like directories below a run root.
target_rule: Target policy caps depth at 4 from the run bucket.
recommended_action: Flatten future lineages to bucket -> child -> functional artifact paths and stop nesting child run IDs.

finding: Observed relative depth reaches 8 levels under one top-level run bucket.
evidence_source: filesystem depth audit performed in this session
current_rule: Same as above.
target_rule: Target policy caps depth at 4 from the run bucket.
recommended_action: Use numbered child folders and local explanation files instead of recursive lineage-within-lineage structures.

finding: A compliant sub-pattern already exists: several child folders use ordinal-plus-cue names such as `01_stage2_remaining10` and `04_stage5_variant_governance_replay`.
current_rule: This is allowed today as an ordered role folder.
target_rule: This matches the target direction for child folders.
recommended_action: Reuse this child-folder convention and remove the nested full child run directory layer for future runs.

finding: Observed run type string is outside the accepted run-type vocabulary.
current_rule: Accepted run types are `intermediate_diagnostic_run`, `component_regression_run`, `full_pipeline_benchmark_run`.
target_rule: Target policy should preserve explicit reproducibility but with one stable vocabulary.
recommended_action: Normalize run-type vocabulary in future specs and document any grandfathered exceptions.

finding: Current builder and validator hardcode the old full run ID format.
current_rule: Generate and validate `run_YYYYMMDD_HHMM_<hash>_<subset>_<stage>_v<version>`.
target_rule: Target bucket is `YYYYMMDD_<short_hash>` and child meaning moves out of folder name.
recommended_action: Refactor into a bucket parser plus child-folder grammar, keeping a compatibility reader for historical names.

finding: Current latest-pointer helper positionally parses old run IDs with `parts[4]` and `parts[5:]`.
current_rule: Assumes old token positions inside full run ID.
target_rule: Target scheme removes those positional tokens from the bucket name.
recommended_action: Stop deriving subset/stage from path tokens; store explicit metadata fields instead.

finding: Current preflight always generates old-style IDs and writes them to `runs/latest.txt`.
current_rule: Old-style run ID generation plus same-day reuse logic.
target_rule: Target scheme needs bucket creation and child-sequence logic instead.
recommended_action: Replace with bucket-aware creation logic and explicit child-step allocation.

finding: Active authority resolution rejects non-old-style `active_run_id` values.
current_rule: `ACTIVE_RUN.json.active_run_id` must satisfy old run ID regex and equal basename of `active_run_dir`.
target_rule: Target scheme still needs strict authority but not the old regex.
recommended_action: Change validation to accept the new bucket identifier and pin exact child artifact paths explicitly.

finding: Lineage audit groups by `run_<date>_<time>_<hash>` prefix, which will not work under the target bucket scheme.
current_rule: Old lineage prefix parser.
target_rule: Target grouping should be by bucket name and child ordinal directories.
recommended_action: Rewrite grouping logic around bucket IDs and child folders.

finding: Top-level semantics audit currently depends on old run ID validity and lineage prefix parsing.
current_rule: Current script ignores or misclassifies non-matching names.
target_rule: Target policy requires exact bucket classification.
recommended_action: Update the script to classify bucket IDs, archive roots, and review surfaces under the new grammar.
